steps.title: Translate the words into inequalities
steps.content: "At least 18" means $x \geq 18$. "Of the 24 scheduled meetings" caps the possible attendance at $24$, so $x \leq 24$.

steps.title: Combine into a compound inequality
steps.content: Both conditions hold at once, so $$18 \leq x \leq 24.$$

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• $x \le 18$ — allows values below the required minimum (e.g., $x=5$). Incorrect.
  • $x \ge 42$ — only $42$ meetings can't exist when just $24$ are scheduled. Incorrect.
  • The matching choice: $18 \le x \le 24$ — matches both "at least $18$" and the cap of $24$ scheduled meetings. Correct.
  • $24 \le x \le 42$ — forces $x$ above the total number of meetings. Incorrect.
C is correct.
